exemplar.title: To what extent has demographic  segmentation marketing  strategy  contributed to Toyotaâs Motor Corporation  market dominance in the automotive industry in North America?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent has demographic  segmentation marketing  strategy  contributed to Toyotaâs Motor Corporation  market dominance in the automotive industry in North America?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Correct identification and definition of âChangeâ as the key concept
Clear analytical links between the concept of Change and Toyotaâs JIT and kaizen initiatives
Strong integration of Change in lifecycle extension discussion for Lexus LS
weaknesses: Concept of Change not consistently woven into every analytical section
Some sections (e.g., Porter grid) revert to descriptive listing rather than concept integration
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band-4 performance demonstrates a strong understanding with clear analytical thinking and partial integration throughout. The student anchors analysis in Toyotaâs operations and links Change to R&D and cost reductions. Occasional lapses into description prevent band-5, so a score of 4 is appropriate.

strengths: Includes three relevant supporting documents (marketâshare chart, BCG matrix, Porterâs table)
Figures are clearly labelled and directly feed into subsequent analysis
weaknesses: Depth of data is limitedâno primary data sources or deeper breakdowns
Citation formatting issues (e.g., malformed citations) reduce clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band-3 evidence of relevance and sufficient depth is apparent, but limited depth and missing primary data justify a step back to band-2. Three documents meet quantity requirements, but the lack of depth warrants 2 marks.

strengths: Adequate selection of recognized tools (BCG, PLC, Porter) covering key strategic dimensions
Applications to Toyota examples (e.g., Lexus LS as a Star) are generally clear
weaknesses: Rationale for tool choice (e.g., demographic segmentation focus) is not fully explained
Applications sometimes remain descriptive with limited critical comparison
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band-3 selection and application are present, but superficial relevance and lack of critical justification justify awarding 2 marks. The toolkit is balanced but not effectively tailored or critically applied.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Uses multiple quantitative data points (unit sales, market share) drawn from reputable sources
Comparative figures for Corolla and RAV4 effectively illustrate demographic targeting
weaknesses: Some data lack context or timeliness
Analysis often stops at description without exploring strategic implications or limitations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band-3 performance shows adequate selection and clear analysis of the research question. Data are used appropriately, but the absence of deeper evaluative insight prevents higher marks, so 3 is awarded.

strengths: Conclusions restate key findings and explicitly answer the research question
Consistent alignment with evidence (market share, cost leadership)
weaknesses: Lacks critical discussion of alternative explanations
Final summary is repetitive rather than integrative
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band-3 criteria are largely met, but limited critical depth and repetition reduce the overall impact of the conclusion. A score of 2 reflects solid consistency without full evaluative sophistication.

strengths: Clear overall organization with title page, contents, methodology, findings, conclusion, references
Appropriate use of headings and tables enhances readability
weaknesses: Table of contents lacks page numbers for quick navigation
Some paragraphs end abruptly, interrupting flow
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report exhibits appropriate structure throughout, matching band-2 descriptors. Minor organizational refinements are needed, but the overall framework is sound.

strengths: All required HL elements (title page, word count, references, supporting documents) are present
weaknesses: Multiple typographical and formatting errors weaken professionalism
Inconsistent reference formatting and missing URLs reduce presentation quality
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although all required elements are included (band-2), numerous typographical and formatting issues demonstrate only basic presentation. A score of 1 reflects the need for substantial polish.

strandSelections.reasoning: A logical structure is evidentâtitle page, contents, methodology, findings, conclusion, referencesâfacilitating reader navigation. Headings and tables are used appropriately, so the work meets the descriptor for âappropriate structure throughout,â band 2.

All included documents relate directly to Toyotaâs market position and strategic choices. They provide numerical data (market share, sales) and strategic frameworks that feed the analysis. Depth is sufficient for discussion but not especially richâfigures are headline numbers with limited breakdownâso the work fits the rubric description of "relevant and sufficiently in-depth," corresponding to band 3.
All required HL elements (title page with RQ and word count, body â¤ 1800 words, references, and supporting documents) are present. No element appears missing, so band 2 is awarded.

Three principal supporting documents are evident: a global market-share bar chart, a BCG matrix table, and a Porter four-quadrant table. This meets the 3-5 requirement. Band 3 is selected because the count sits comfortably in range without any excess or deficit.

Each tool is applied to Toyota examples (e.g., Lexus LS as a Star, Sequoia as a Question Mark, cost-leadership evidence in Corolla pricing). The relevance to the demographic-segmentation research question is generally clear, yet the applications are sometimes descriptive and omit critical comparison with rivals or alternative strategies. This satisfies âadequately applied with relevance not always clear,â matching band 3.

The IA uses multiple data pointsâunit sales, market-share percentages, growth ratesâsourced from Statista and Toyota press releases. The figures are incorporated into tables and narrative, indicating **adequate selection and use**. Some data are dated or lack context (e.g., Lexus vs. BMW volumes), so effectiveness is not âsufficientâ or âeffectiveâ but comfortably meets band 3.

Analysis goes beyond description by linking demographic targets to portfolio position and cost strategies, yet often stops short of evaluating significance or exploring limitations. Integration across sections is intermittent. This corresponds to âsome analysis of the research questionââband 3.
See above: the research question is directly addressed, and the response is grounded in cited data, justifying a band 3 rating for the âanswerâ strand.

The student selects three recognised toolsâthe BCG Matrix, Product Life Cycle, and Porterâs Generic Strategiesâcovering portfolio, product, and competitive dimensions. This constitutes an **adequate and balanced** toolkit but lacks any more advanced or niche frameworks. Hence band 3 for âadequate selection.â

The conclusion restates key findingsâmarket share figures, cost leadership effectsâand explicitly answers the research question by asserting that demographic segmentation âhugely impactedâ dominance. Assertions align with evidence previously cited, though critical discussion of alternative causes is limited. This fulfils âconclusions fully consistent with evidence and explicitly answer the question,â earning band 3.

Throughout the report, the student anchors discussion firmly in Toyotaâs North-American operationsâsales figures, specific models, production plants and pricing tacticsâshowing a clear understanding of how demographic segmentation applies to the chosen organisation. Integration is visible in the BCG matrix, PLC comments and Porter analysis. Yet, linkages sometimes lapse into listing facts rather than analysing their effect on the firm, so the connection is strong but not comprehensive, meriting band 4.

The student correctly identifies Change as the key concept and explains it in the context of Toyotaâs JIT, kaizen and product-life-cycle extension strategies. References to R&D driving demographic segmentation and to operational changes that lowered costs demonstrate a solid conceptual grasp. However, the idea of Change is not woven through every section; some paragraphs (e.g., Porter grid) revert to description without explicitly linking back to Change. This represents **strong but only partially integrated analysis**, aligning with band 4.

annotations.comment: The student correctly identifies âChangeâ as the key concept, providing a solid conceptual foundation for the analysis.
The research question is clearly stated and focused, guiding the entire investigation effectively.
The table of contents provides a logical overview of sections, facilitating easy navigation through the report.
Consider adding page numbers to each entry in the table of contents to improve usability and reader orientation.
Figure I effectively presents global marketâshare data, setting a strong empirical foundation for subsequent analysis.
The citation âSD \bar{I}â appears malformed; revise citation formatting to ensure clarity and traceability of the source.
Integrate the concept of Change more explicitly in the background section to reinforce the reportâs conceptual cohesion.
Link the definition of demographic segmentation directly to Toyotaâs North American strategy to strengthen relevance.
The definition of Change is accurate but would benefit from explicit examples of process adjustments at Toyota.
This paragraph ends abruptly, leaving the reader without a complete thought; ensure every point is fully developed.
The methodology section concisely outlines the chosen toolsâBCG, PLC, and Porterâs frameworksâfor analysis.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: No primary data sources are specified; consider detailing interviews, surveys, or questionnaires to strengthen research validity.
Typographical error âs Internalâ; proofread for grammatical accuracy to maintain professional presentation.
Explain why the BCG Matrix is particularly suited to evaluating demographic segmentation rather than general portfolio management.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Figure II is clearly labelled and presents Toyotaâs product positions effectively for strategic analysis.
The matrix discussion is largely descriptive; deepen evaluation by recommending strategic actions for Question Marks and Dogs.
The discussion of lifecycle extension for Lexus LS demonstrates strong integration of the key concept of Change.
Use of precise sales figures for Lexus LS strengthens the evidence base and supports analytical claims.
Conclude the discussion of Sequoiaâs USP by explicitly linking it to market dominance outcomes.
Comparative market-share data for Corolla and RAV4 is used effectively to illustrate demographic segmentation success.
The final synthesis repeats earlier observations; combine insights into a more concise, integrated summary.
The Porterâs generic strategy table is comprehensive and clearly organized, facilitating comparative analysis.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The differentiation focus paragraph ends mid-sentence; ensure all thoughts are fully articulated.
Linking of cost leadership practices to specific Toyota initiatives effectively grounds theoretical discussion.
The conclusion reiterates key findings and explicitly answers the research question, maintaining consistency with evidence.
Numerous typos (e.g., âdistrintâ) appear in the conclusion; perform a thorough proofread for professional polish.
The clear labelling of the Conclusion section enhances structure, guiding the reader to final insights.
Break the lengthy concluding paragraph into shorter segments or bullet points to improve readability.
References are inconsistently formatted; unify entries to a single citation style to improve professionalism.
Include DOIs for journal articles to strengthen academic rigor and facilitate source retrieval.
The Pressroom reference on page 12 is missing its URL; provide the full link for verification.
Apply hanging indent formatting to all references to enhance presentation consistency.
Group references by category (websites, press releases, journal articles) to improve organizational clarity.
Ensure all retrieval dates follow the same format (e.g., MM DD, YYYY) to maintain consistency across entries.
